data.body: Plan-review PASS (blocking=0, only a file_impact advisory, remediated via set_file_impact), signed at HEAD ea79ecca; claimed immediately. Implementation design note (key finding for the adapter): there is an IMPEDANCE MISMATCH between epic A's BatchRunner seam and sizing.py. The seam's run(req: BatchRunRequest, agent_runner: AgentStepRunner) hands a WORKFLOW AgentStepRunner (run(ctx: StepContext)->StepResult), but sizing.pass1_with_ladder(runner, cfg, plan, chunk, agentic, events) expects a rebar.llm.Runner + LLMConfig and calls passes.pass1_chunk (plan-review-specific finder). So ProductionBatchRunner cannot just call the seam's agent_runner; it must bridge. Two viable designs: (A) ProductionBatchRunner ignores the seam's agent_runner and constructs/holds its own rebar.llm.Runner + LLMConfig (from the BatchRunRequest's workflow/repo_root), driving pass1_with_ladder + shed_to_budget + checkpointing directly, and journals an enforced batch_plan (enforced:True, ladder events, shed list); OR (B) B2 injects a Runner-backed AgentStepRunner adapter and the production runner calls through it — but pass1_with_ladder's per-criterion escalation + size-ladder don't fit the one-call AgentStepRunner shape, so (A) is cleaner. sizing.py stays the logic home (no algorithm duplication); the runner is a thin orchestration adapter mapping BatchRunRequest.criteria/model_ladder/usd_budget -> chunks/cfg/cap. Equivalence tests should pin: batch packing, size-ladder escalation (batch->1-crit->model-ladder->too-big), shed-to-budget ordering (overlay->container->core), and checkpoint resume by material fingerprint. Status: claimed + planned + file_impact set, on branch feat/production-batch-runner; implementation not yet started.
